file_insights.summary: Comprehensive technical summary documenting migration from polling-based to event-driven dispatcher architecture using notify-debouncer-mini crate
key_sections: Implementation details across 3 files (dispatcher.rs, merge_gate.rs, Cargo.toml)
ASCII architectural diagrams comparing polling vs event-driven loops

patterns_discovered.usage: Clear tabular presentation of metrics across 4 dimensions (avg latency, CPU when idle, debounce window, max latency)
Visual representation of polling loop vs event-driven select! pattern with annotations
patterns_discovered.effectiveness: Aids conceptual understanding of architectural shift

patterns_discovered.usage: 7 checkmarked criteria tied to implementation requirements
patterns_discovered.effectiveness: Provides verification trail and customer acceptance proof

patterns_discovered.usage: Unit, integration, and release build verification with specific test counts
Outlines 3 potential improvements (metrics, dynamic debounce, watchdog fallback)
patterns_discovered.effectiveness: Provides roadmap and acknowledges known limitations

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Complete file replacement rather than incremental edit
gotchas_discovered.context: Subtask required documenting a completely different feature (event-driven vs polling), so wholesale replacement of result.md was appropriate rather than merge/patch
Result.md documents that `--poll-ms` flag is preserved for backward compatibility when filesystem events fail, which is critical for CI/container environments

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created comprehensive technical documentation summarizing event-driven dispatcher refactoring with quantified performance improvements
key_achievements: Complete traceability to implementation files and changes
All 7 acceptance criteria verified and documented
465 tests confirmed passing with no regressions
Architectural benefits explained with ASCII diagrams
Backward compatibility and fallback modes documented

recommendations.suggestion: Add specific timing measurements from actual test runs (e.g., 'measured latency: 12ms' vs 'typical 10-30ms') in a footnote or appendix
recommendations.rationale: Would provide empirical validation of performance claims

recommendations.suggestion: Implement the prometheus/OpenTelemetry metrics suggested in 'Future Enhancements' section to measure real-world latency
recommendations.rationale: Enables continuous performance validation in production

recommendations.suggestion: Add watchdog timer that falls back to polling if filesystem events stop arriving (mentioned in Future Enhancements)
recommendations.rationale: Handles edge cases in network filesystems or unreliable environments

recommendations.suggestion: Document any platform-specific behavior differences (macOS FSEvents vs Linux inotify) in deployment guide
recommendations.rationale: Prevents surprises when running on different operating systems
